title: Bound and compact fresh open-PR observation for what-now
goal: Reduce what-now latency and output volume without weakening the fresh fail-closed open pull-request claim guard.

acceptance.assertion: Distinct GitHub repositories are observed concurrently through the existing fresh gh pr list path; no cache or search-index snapshot weakens claim safety.
Concurrency is bounded and configurable, duplicate repository observations are deduplicated, and one repository failure remains a fail-closed blocker only for affected resources.
what-now offers a compact projection that retains decision and blocker truth while omitting high-volume evidence details; full output remains available by default.
A live before/after benchmark records frontier latency and compact JSON size on the exact reviewed head.
Focused tests and complete Bureau validation pass on the exact reviewed head.

metadata.safety_boundary: Do not cache or use eventually consistent search results for claim-time PR truth. Preserve per-repository fail-closed diagnostics and configured PR count cap.
does_not_establish: absence of GitHub API latency variance
metadata.outcome: Bureau PR #631 merged bounded concurrent fresh open-PR observation and an opt-in compact what-now projection. The implementation is present in the current immutable Bureau runtime and passed a fresh live compact readback during closeout.
validation: PR #631 is merged at exact head b4b038da493536a04d13c0c9a2eb2d1ef59f5da0 into merge commit 770597460620b4bb6f7cea7bae577dfc8a0931ff.
Validate on Python 3.10 and 3.12, CodeQL actions, CodeQL python and aggregate CodeQL all succeeded on the exact implementation head.
The merged implementation diff reconstructed from first parent 363d932a3054dc71004a1bdd0e4c66fb91cbffd7 is 24,111 bytes with SHA-256 5e2350b423564802170b405d0d2f2401128c6ca9781a35ad885357199c4f107e.
The initial exact-head benchmark already recorded in this task measured 1.320424 seconds and 8,699 bytes over 17 repository resources, a 20.14x speedup over the 26.587472-second sequential baseline.

Current immutable runtime source d2a3d1da948c8356ce9277a321392d6f0f21d61c contains the implementation merge and completed a fresh compact readback in 2.19446 seconds with 14,344 output bytes and no stderr.

verified_acceptance.fresh-parallel-observation: Distinct repositories are deduplicated and observed concurrently through the existing fresh gh pr list path; no cache or eventually consistent search index was introduced.
verified_acceptance.bounded-fail-closed: Worker concurrency is bounded and configurable, duplicate repositories are deduplicated, and repository observation failures remain fail-closed for affected resources.
verified_acceptance.compact-projection: The compact projection preserves selection and aggregate blocker truth while the full default projection remains available.
verified_acceptance.measured-effect: The exact implementation head recorded 1.320424 seconds and 8,699 bytes versus a 26.587472-second baseline; current immutable-runtime readback also completed successfully.
verified_acceptance.full-validation: All required GitHub checks passed on the exact implementation head, and the current immutable runtime passed a fresh live readback.

tradeoffs: Fresh repository observations create a bounded burst of concurrent gh processes in exchange for lower wall-clock latency.
Compact output is intentionally lossy and opt-in; consumers requiring complete evidence must use the default projection.
Observed latency remains dependent on GitHub and network variance; the measured multiplier is not a fixed service-level guarantee.
